sections.paragraph: m. that which is left behind (when one dies). Pro 31:8, בְּנֵי חֲלֹוף “children left behind,” orphans (Arab. خلف II. to leave children when dying, Mar 12:19, 20 Act 18:21). [“A going away (see the root חָלַף No. 1 ), especially when others are left behind, hence the death of parents. Arab. خلف to leave children at death.”]
